summary: Recent changes to Firefox's libwebrtc integration focus on enhancing video and audio handling capabilities in real-time communications. Key improvements include the ability to effectively parse VP8 and VP9 quantization parameters for better video quality assessment, and a cleanup of deprecated functions, which ensures the WebRTC platform remains current and efficient. Furthermore, there is a shift towards safer memory management practices, as seen in the adoption of `std::unique_ptr` for various decoder and encoder creations. Enhancements to the handling of H.264 video standards and better accuracy in assessing media capabilities based on resolution and framerate are also significant. Together, these updates streamline performance and improve the reliability of video calls, making **real-time communication clearer and more efficient for users**.
